(**SAD**) is a regional historical [geodetic datum](/wiki/Geodetic_datum "Geodetic datum") for [South America](/wiki/South_America "South America").

first_paragraph: The **South American Datum** (**SAD**) is a regional historical [geodetic datum](/wiki/Geodetic_datum "Geodetic datum") for [South America](/wiki/South_America "South America"). The most common version was established in 1969 (*SAD69*), as adopted by the [Pan American Institute of Geography and History](/wiki/Pan_American_Institute_of_Geography_and_History "Pan American Institute of Geography and History").[[1]](#cite_note-Fischer1970-1)

The **South American Datum** (**SAD**) is a regional historical [geodetic datum](/wiki/Geodetic_datum "Geodetic datum") for [South America](/wiki/South_America "South America").
The most common version was established in 1969 (*SAD69*), as adopted by the [Pan American Institute of Geography and History](/wiki/Pan_American_Institute_of_Geography_and_History "Pan American Institute of Geography and History").[[1]](#cite_note-Fischer1970-1)

It uses as [reference ellipsoid](/wiki/Reference_ellipsoid "Reference ellipsoid") the [Geodetic Reference System](/wiki/Geodetic_Reference_System "Geodetic Reference System") 1967 (GRS-67), recommended by the [International Union of Geodesy and Geophysics](/wiki/International_Union_of_Geodesy_and_Geophysics "International Union of Geodesy and Geophysics") in [Lucerne](/wiki/Lucerne "Lucerne") in 1967.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
This ellipsoid acquired topocentric orientation defined at the [astrogeodetic](/wiki/Astrogeodesy "Astrogeodesy") [vertex](/wiki/Geodetic_mark "Geodetic mark") *Chuá*, in the municipality of [Uberaba](/wiki/Uberaba "Uberaba"), Brazil.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

This datum was subsequently adopted by many South American countries, including the earlier *Brazilian Geodetic System* (*Sistema Geodésico Brasileiro* - *SGB*).
In most countries, SAD was recently replaced by [SIRGAS](/w/index.php?title=SIRGAS&action=edit&redlink=1 "SIRGAS (page does not exist)"); for example, in Brazil SIRGAS was adopted starting in 2005 and mandated since 2014.[[5]](#cite_note-IBGE_2005-5)
